caption: What if a liquidity pool could play the World Cup?
This is Mundial — a Uniswap v4 hook on X Layer. It doesn't pick one match or trust an admin. It runs the entire tournament — with no one in charge.
Fans pledge one of eight teams — once, irreversibly. When their team's match is live, every swap becomes a shot on goal. Volume scores goals. The market plays the football.
Watch it run. Argentina versus France — fans trade, goals go up.
Brazil against England is scoreless into extra time. One Brazilian fan swaps… GOLDEN GOAL.
The match settles inside the very swap that scored it.
Semifinals, penalties decided by shot counts, a final — and a champion is crowned. All of it, hook storage.
One dynamic-fee pool. Fans pay lower fees — a quarter percent, dropping to point-one-five while their team plays.
A zero-point-two percent skim builds the Champions Pot. Winning fans claim it pro-rata; leftovers go to the LPs.
No owner. No oracle. No randomness. The only inputs are swaps and time. Seventy-seven tests. Fuzzed.
Deployed on X Layer. Code public on GitHub.
Mundial. Live on X Layer. The pool that plays the World Cup.
